person.bio: Anish Bista is a working professional with expertise in DevOps, cloud technologies, Kubernetes, Golang and the broader cloud-native ecosystem. He is the youngest Kubestronaut from Nepal and an active contributor to the cloud-native and open-source communities. Anish was previously involved with CNCF Kathmandu, where he played a significant role in growing and strengthening the community to its current state. He has contributed to multiple CNCF projects, including KubeVirt and Kanister, and is currently leading the process of donating the KubeVirtBMC project, further demonstrating his commitment to the CNCF and open-source ecosystem.

person.name: Krishna Kumar Uvanachandran
person.bio: Krishna Uvanachandran is a Lead DevOps Engineer with experience in designing, implementing, and managing robust infrastructure solutions for high-traffic web applications. He holds a Master's degree in Computer Science and has continuously pursued professional development through certifications such as Microsoft Certified DevOps Engineer and Kubernetes Administrator.  As a DevOps Lead, Krishna excels in fostering a collaborative environment where cross-functional teams can thrive. He is known for his strategic vision in aligning DevOps practices with business objectives, driving innovation, and ensuring compliance with industry standards and best practices.

person.name: Miguel Angel Vila Huallpa
person.bio: Graduated in Network Administration, due to unusual circumstances, I ended up working as a developer and sysadmin. Since 2019, I have been working with container technologies. I enjoy automating processes with various tools and configuring monitoring of various platforms and applications.

person.name: Sachin K Vaikan
person.bio: Sachin K Vaikan is a Senior DevOps Engineer and Cloud Native enthusiast with over 10 years of experience. Passionate about cutting-edge technologies, he specializes in architecting scalable, resilient platforms on Kubernetes.

person.name: Sameer Mene
person.bio: A Cloud Solution Architect based in Pune, India, with a passion for cloud-native technologies, DevOps practices, and automation. Guided by the mantra, "If you do it more than twice, automate it!" I focus on simplifying workflows and building resilient systems. With expertise in Java, Go, and Python, I have designed and implemented solutions for Kubernetes cluster management, service mesh, operators, IAM, storage and edge computing. I specialize in crafting cloud-agnostic solutions, leveraging tools like Terraform and Pulumi to automate deployments and streamline operations across diverse environments. With a solid background in the telecom domain and a keen interest in emerging technologies such as 5G, my expertise also extends to AI, large language models (LLMs), and the dynamic landscape of Web 3.0.

person.name: Takahiro Fukushima
person.bio: I'm a software engineer specializing in cloud architecture, infrastructure construction, and DevOps. I'm passionate about platform engineering and have expertise in building development platforms using containers. I'm always keeping up with the latest trends in Kubernetes.

person.name: Do Anh Quan
person.bio: I am a Software Engineer dedicated to designing and delivering applications that are both scalable and highly resilient. My primary focus is tackling complex computational challenges and utilizing my expertise in DevOps and Cloud technologies to significantly improve developer experience. I have a particular passion for solving intricate problems related to containerization and Kubernetes orchestration.

person.name: Anastasiya Hashnikava
person.bio: A Bachelor’s degree in Mathematics forms my educational background. I enjoy structure, automation and systems that are both efficient and secure. The sea & sunshine inspire me and I also value meaningful communication with interesting people.

person.name: Chris Aniszczyk
person.bio: Chris brings more than 15 years experience as The Linux Foundation’s vice president of Developer Programs. His work is focused on working with developer community to advance open source projects at scale. Previously Chris served as Twitter's head of open source, where he led a team of developer advocates and was responsible for Twitter's open source engineering, strategy and culture. He has also contributed to Gentoo Linux and served on the Eclipse Foundation's Board of Directors and the Java Community Process Executive Committee. An engineer by trade, Chris brings a passion for both open source and community development to the organization.

person.name: Ishan Tripathi
person.bio: I'm a Staff Software Engineer with 8+ years of experience in full-stack software development and building modern cloud-native platforms. My core expertise lies in architecting secure, scalable systems using technologies like PHP, Laravel, Node.js, and JavaScript, while integrating DevOps best practices and infrastructure automation using AWS, Kubernetes, and Terraform. I'm certified across 13 domains, including 7X AWS, 5X Kubernetes, and 1X Terraform, and I focus on delivering resilient, high-performance systems that balance code quality, security, and operational efficiency. In recent years, I’ve expanded into the CNCF ecosystem, working on platform engineering initiatives, CI/CD pipelines, and internal developer tooling that bridge the gap between development and infrastructure. I’m passionate about solving real-world engineering challenges, mentoring teams, and driving continuous improvement across the software delivery lifecycle.

person.name: Jake Pineda
person.bio: Jake is an Membership Sales Representative at The Linux Foundation, working with the Cloud Native Computing Foundation. He brings experience in technology sales and cloud solutions, with an academic background that includes an Associate degree in Computer Science - Network Technology from Lehigh Carbon Community College, and ongoing studies for a Bachelor's in Information Technology at Western Governors University. Jake is dedicated to enhancing open-source software solutions in cloud native computing and network security.

person.name: Mangale Lopchan
person.bio: I'm a passionate Platform Engineer dedicated to building and scaling reliable systems using CNCF technologies. I share my journey and tutorials on my YouTube channel, Linux Guy Nepal, where I dive deep into Linux, Kubernetes, and cloud infrastructure. Driven by a desire for open source collaboration, I am constantly exploring new ways to enhance developer experience and operational efficiency.

person.name: Marius Tanawa Tsamo
person.bio: I have a Master's degree in Systems, Network, and Security. Although I'm very passionate about container environments, I'm even more passionate about meaningful contributions. I'm familiar with most of the graduated projects in the CNCF landscape. ArgoCD, Istio, Cilium ect..

person.name: Mohamed Nejib Zayet
person.bio: Cloud and Kubernetes enthusiast focused on building simple, scalable, and reliable platforms. As a FinOps Certified Professional, I balance performance and cost to deliver real business value. I’m a continuous learner and pragmatic problem solver with strong experience in demanding environments.

person.name: Yin Zhang
person.bio: I work as a Support Engineer specializing in AKS. I’m passionate about continuous learning and always eager to support others in solving technical challenges. I enjoy collaborating with teams to deliver reliable solutions and improve service experiences.

person.name: Ersin Sari
person.bio: I am a Cloud Architect and DevOps/DevSecOps Consultant specializing in Kubernetes-based platforms, GitOps workflows, observability stacks, and automated cloud infrastructure. I help organizations build secure, scalable, and resilient cloud-native systems by combining best practices in automation, platform engineering, and operational excellence. I enjoy turning complex problems into simple, repeatable solutions and sharing real-world lessons learned through in-depth articles on Medium.

person.name: Fabian Dohmen
person.bio: I work as a principal consultant in the AWS field and focus on the infrastructure side. My primary stack mostly contains AWS, CNCF projects and the hashicorp products.

person.name: Hyungchan Cho
person.bio: Hello, I’m currently leading ALM and DevOps engineering initiatives at Hyundai Mobis, where I focus on driving innovation in areas such as CI/CD and cloud-native transformation.  For over three years, my group and I have designed, built, and operated a hybrid cloud infrastructure that seamlessly integrates an on-premises Kubernetes cluster with Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) — achieving an optimal balance of scalability and cost efficiency.  My core expertise lies in Kubernetes operations, security, and observability, ensuring resilient and transparent platform management across complex environments.  I’m also deeply passionate about Generative AI technologies, particularly AI-powered code assistants, Model Context Protocol (MCP), and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) — exploring how these can further elevate developer productivity and system intelligence.

person.name: Sergei Kubin
person.bio: Sergei is an ICT Engineer with over 20 years of experience in enterprise hardware and software support. His primary focus area is data storage systems. He is an IBM RedBooks author with more than 10 published books.

person.name: Venkatesh Ravichandran
person.bio: Venkatesh Ravichandran is a Senior Platform Engineer with 7+ years of experience specializing in Kubernetes, cloud infrastructure, and distributed systems. He has strong expertise in container orchestration, security, and cloud-native architecture, with a focus on building scalable and reliable platforms. He is passionate about automating infrastructure, designing robust systems, and sharing knowledge through technical blogs and talks.

person.name: Azar Karimli
person.bio: I am a Linux/DevOps Engineer focused on Kubernetes, Linux infrastructure, automation, and cloud-native platforms. My key interests in the CNCF ecosystem include Kubernetes operations, security, observability, and building reliable production environments. I enjoy solving real-world infrastructure challenges and continuously improving my expertise in modern platform engineering. Being part of the cloud-native community motivates me to keep learning, sharing, and growing.
